title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Colds
intro: Herbal teas for Colds are a natural remedy made from various plant extracts, such as leaves, flowers, roots, and bark, that are used to alleviate the symptoms of the common cold. These teas are effective in treating colds due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to boost the immune system and reduce congestion, cough, and fever. Some popular herbal teas used to treat colds include Echinacea tea, which stimulates the immune system and fights off infections; Peppermint tea, which eases congestion and cough; Ginger tea, which reduces inflammation and soothes a sore throat; Eucalyptus tea, which helps to loosen mucus and relieve congestion; Lemon and Honey tea, which provides hydration and soothes a sore throat; and Thyme tea, which has antimicrobial properties that help to combat infections. By incorporating these teas into your daily routine, you can help to alleviate the symptoms of the common cold and promote overall health and well-being.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to help alleviate the symptoms of colds and flu, thanks to their immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat the colds ailment by stimulating the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms, and protecting against infections. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea teas, including alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polysaccharides, contribute to its therapeutic effects by enhancing the production of white blood cells and activating natural killer cells. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat colds include reduced congestion, fever, and sore throat, as well as improved overall well-being and a shorter recovery time.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the Echinacea mixture and add it to 1 cup of boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup. Discard the solids.
Drink the tea warm, up to 3 times a day, when needed for cold relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided study, Echinacea purpurea teas for colds have been shown to reduce the total number of cold episodes, days with colds, and cold episodes requiring additional medication.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be effective in treating colds, but some users may experience side effects such as nausea, stomach upset, or allergic reactions like hives or itching, particularly when consumed in large amounts or for extended periods. To minimize potential risks, users should start with small doses, gradually increase as needed, and be cautious of interactions with other medications or health conditions, such as high blood pressure or allergies.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Echinacea purpurea tea for colds.
Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 3 times a day.
Use 2-3 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea per cup of boiling water.
Continue usage for 5-7 days to support immune system.
Monitor symptoms and adjust usage or consult doctor if necessary.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas are a popular herbal remedy used to treat colds and flu due to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol and eucalyptol present in this herbal preparation help to relieve congestion and open up airways, making it easier to breathe. Bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes contribute to its antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which aid in fighting off the underlying infection. By using Eucalyptus globulus teas, individuals can benefit from its ability to reduce coughing, fever, and body aches, ultimately promoting faster recovery from colds and flu.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water.
Take 5-7 fresh eucalyptus leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried eucalyptus leaves.
Add the eucalyptus leaves to 1 cup of boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the leaves.
Drink the tea hot or cold, 2-3 times a day to help relieve cold symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM", Eucalyptus globulus teas for colds are mentioned as one of the top-cited plant species used in the treatment of respiratory tract infections (RTIs), with a frequency of citation (FOC) of 6.8%.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can help alleviate cold symptoms, but possible side effects may include digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions when using this herbal preparation include avoiding use in children and pregnant women, as well as taking it in moderation to prevent over-stimulation and potential respiratory issues.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea 3-4 times a day
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the tea
Continue usage for 3-5 days or until symptoms improve
Consult a doctor if symptoms persist or worsen after usage

preparation_amazon.title: Jovvily Eucalyptus Leaves - 1lb - Dried - Cut & Sifted - Herbal Tea

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat the common cold and its associated symptoms such as congestion and cough. The properties of this herbal preparation help to treat the cold by reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and loosening mucus, making it easier to expel. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, possess anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help to alleviate cold symptoms. The benefits of using ginger tea to treat the cold include its ability to soothe a sore throat, reduce congestion, and promote relaxation, making it a popular natural remedy for cold relief.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried ginger root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Grate the dried ginger root to release its flavor and aroma.
Add the grated ginger to the boiling water and stir well.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to combine.
Strain the tea and drink it while it's still warm to help relieve cold symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Pharmaceuticals (Basel, Switzerland)", Zingiber officinale teas for colds may be used as a therapy or complementary self-treatment, relieving symptoms such as fever, nasal congestion, and sneezing.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be beneficial in alleviating cold symptoms, but they may cause stomach upset and heartburn in some individuals, particularly those with pre-existing digestive issues, due to its spicy and warming properties. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to consume Zingiber officinale teas in moderation, especially on an empty stomach, and to avoid taking it with other spicy or acidic foods.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes before straining and drinking.
Continue usage for 3-5 days or as symptoms subside.
Consult a healthcare professional if symptoms persist or worsen.

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas, also known as elderberry tea, have been traditionally used to treat colds and flu due to their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids, help to combat the infection by inhibiting the replication of the virus and reducing inflammation in the body. By consuming elderberry tea, individuals can experience relief from cold symptoms such as fever, cough, and sore throat, and may also benefit from reduced duration and severity of the illness. The benefits of using elderberry tea to treat colds include its natural and non-invasive approach, making it a popular choice among those seeking a holistic remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Sambucus nigra flowers, or 1 teaspoon of dried flowers per cup of boiling water.
Combine the flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan or teapot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the flavors to release.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and drink while warm to help soothe a cold.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Phytotherapy research : PTR", Sambucus nigra teas for colds have been shown to contain antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which may be beneficial in treating viral infections, including influenza.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can be effective in alleviating cold symptoms, but possible side effects include allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, or difficulty breathing, as well as digestive issues, like nausea and stomach cramps. When using Sambucus nigra teas to treat colds, it's essential to take precautions, such as starting with small doses to monitor individual tolerance and potential interactions with other medications, and being cautious when consuming Sambucus nigra teas with certain health conditions, like high blood pressure.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Sambucus nigra tea 3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried flowers per cup of boiling water.
Increase dose to 2 teaspoons if symptoms persist after 48 hours.
Use Sambucus nigra tea for 5-7 days for cold relief.
Consult a healthcare professional if symptoms worsen or persist.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the roots of the licorice plant, have been used to treat colds and other respiratory ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help to soothe a sore throat, reduce congestion, and alleviate coughs. By reducing inflammation and promoting the expulsion of mucus, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas help to relieve symptoms associated with colds, such as a runny nose and cough. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat colds include reduced severity and duration of symptoms, improved respiratory function, and a natural alternative to over-the-counter medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a strainer or tea infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the dried root in the strainer or tea infuser.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the root to release its flavor and benefits.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the root. Add honey to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate cold symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Mini reviews in medicinal chemistry", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for colds may be beneficial due to their ability to modulate the immune system, inhibit virus growth, produce anti-inflammatory activity, and inactivate viruses.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat colds, but consuming them excessively may lead to side effects such as increased blood pressure, water retention, and low potassium levels. To use these teas safely, it is recommended to consume them in moderation, ideally for a short duration, and to avoid drinking them at bedtime to minimize potential disruptions in sleep patterns.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried roots per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to allow roots to infuse.
Take 1 cup in the morning, afternoon, and evening.
Continue for 3-5 days or until cold symptoms subside.

preparation_amazon.title: Pukka Herbal Teas Licorice and Cinnamon - 20 Bags, 20 Count

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been used for centuries to treat colds due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat colds by reducing congestion, relieving coughs, and soothing a sore throat. Thymus vulgaris tea contains bioactive constituents such as thymol, carvacrol, and rosmarinic acid, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi, thus helping to alleviate symptoms of colds. The benefits of Thymus vulgaris tea in treating colds include its ability to reduce the severity and duration of symptoms, promote a speedy recovery, and provide relief from congestion and coughs.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 2 tablespoons of dried thyme leaves (Thymus vulgaris).
Steep the thyme leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture into a cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if needed. Stir well.
Drink the thyme tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate cold symptoms.
Consult a doctor before using thyme tea for children, pregnant women, or people with allergies.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Pharmaceuticals (Basel, Switzerland)", Thymus vulgaris teas for colds may be used as a complementary self-treatment to relieve symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be beneficial in treating colds, but may cause side effects such as increased heart rate, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin. Precautions should be taken when using Thymus vulgaris teas to treat colds, including not consuming it in excess, avoiding it during pregnancy, and being cautious when combining it with other medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Thymus vulgaris tea 3 times a day.
Take 1 tablespoon of Thymus vulgaris tincture 2 times a day.
Use 5-10 drops of Thymus vulgaris essential oil in a diffuser.
Take Thymus vulgaris tea or tincture for 7-10 days.
Consult a doctor for severe colds and symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, also known as English lavender tea, have been traditionally used to treat colds and respiratory issues. This herbal preparation contains properties such as antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties that help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, thereby reducing congestion and coughing. The bioactive constituents, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which contribute to the tea's ability to combat infections and promote healing. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat colds include its ability to reduce symptoms such as fever, cough, and congestion, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and promote overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Lavandula angustifolia flowers in a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into another cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, for sweetness.
Drink the tea warm, 2-3 times a day, to help relieve cold symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can help alleviate the symptoms of colds, but may cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin or allergies. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, avoid taking it on an empty stomach, and be cautious when consuming it with other medications or substances that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 3 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Take for 3-5 days, or as needed, to relieve cold symptoms.
Monitor symptoms and consult a doctor if they worsen.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat the common cold and flu due to their immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea angustifolia, such as alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polysaccharides, help to stimulate the immune system and reduce the severity of cold symptoms. By consuming Echinacea angustifolia teas, individuals can experience relief from congestion, cough, and fatigue associated with the common cold, as well as a reduced risk of developing secondary infections. The benefits of using Echinacea angustifolia teas to treat colds include reduced symptom duration, improved immune function, and a lower risk of complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Echinacea angustifolia root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the Echinacea root in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Echinacea root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the Echinacea root.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally when you feel symptoms of a cold coming on or when you're already feeling unwell.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology, Echinacea angustifolia teas for colds may be beneficial, but there is a risk of exacerbating allergies and asthma, and potentially causing anaphylactic reactions, despite no common drug interactions reported.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea angustifolia teas can be used to treat colds, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive stomachs or allergies. To avoid potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase it, as well as to avoid taking it for extended periods, especially if you have a history of autoimmune disorders or are prone to kidney problems.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat the common cold and flu due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the severity of symptoms such as congestion, cough, and sore throat. The herbal preparation's ability to thin mucus and promote its expulsion from the body also contributes to its effectiveness in alleviating respiratory issues associated with colds. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, play a crucial role in modulating the immune response and reducing oxidative stress, which helps to combat the underlying causes of the common cold. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas has been shown to provide relief from cold symptoms, reduce the duration of illness, and even help to prevent future episodes of respiratory infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup to remove the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea if desired for sweetness.
Drink the Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve cold symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to American journal of health-system pharmacy : AJHP : official journal of the American Society of Health-System Pharmacists, Ginkgo biloba teas for colds are considered potentially safe.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can help alleviate symptoms of colds, but possible side effects include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, take it in moderation, monitor your body's response, and avoid mixing it with other medications or supplements, as this may enhance its effects and increase the risk of adverse interactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat colds due to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat colds by relieving congestion and reducing the severity of symptoms such as coughing and sneezing, ultimately providing relief and comfort to those affected. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis teas, including rosmarinic acid, caffeic acid, and camphor, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, which help to reduce inflammation and combat the underlying causes of the cold. The benefits of using Salvia officinalis teas to treat colds include a reduction in symptoms, improved respiratory function, and a boost to the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser.
Rinse the Salvia officinalis leaves with cold water and place them in the tea infuser.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves in the tea infuser.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the leaves from the infuser.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate cold symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Salvia officinalis teas for colds may be useful as an adjuvant for the treatment of respiratory infections, as its ethanol extract was found to revert resistance to gentamicin in Staphylococcus aureus.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat colds, but potential side effects include digestive issues, nausea, and stomach upset due to its high thymol content, as well as allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Salvia officinalis teas safely, it's essential to consume them in moderation, brew them correctly to avoid excessive thymol release, and be aware of any adverse reactions, especially if you have a history of stomach problems or allergies.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been used for centuries to treat colds and flu due to their antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including quinine and other alkaloids, help to reduce fever, alleviate congestion, and combat viral infections that cause colds. By acting on the body's immune system and reducing inflammation, Cinchona officinalis teas provide relief from the symptoms of colds, such as sore throats, coughs, and runny noses. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat colds include their natural and non-addictive properties, making them a popular choice for those seeking a holistic approach to managing colds and flu.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Cinchona officinalis bark in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste, if desired, to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve cold symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Data in brief, Cinchona officinalis teas are not mentioned in the study as a treatment for colds, as the study only mentions that 23.2% of the documented species are used to treat colds but does not specify the plant species used for this purpose.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat colds, but they may cause side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. Precautions should be taken when using Cinchona officinalis teas, including avoiding consumption by pregnant or breastfeeding women, as well as individuals with high blood pressure or other cardiovascular conditions, and monitoring blood pressure and other vital signs closely.

The benefits of using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat colds include reduced congestion, sinus pressure, and coughing, as well as improved overall respiratory function.

Combine the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a pot.

Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help relieve cold symptoms.

The bioactive constituents of goldenseal, including berberine and hydrastine, help to reduce congestion and soothe a sore throat by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause colds. By reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system, goldenseal tea helps to alleviate symptoms of colds, such as coughing, sneezing, and fatigue. The benefits of using goldenseal tea to treat colds include its natural and non-addictive properties, making it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic approach to managing respiratory health.

Boil 1 cup of water in a pot. Remove from heat when boiling.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried Hydrastis canadensis roots to the boiling water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth. Discard the solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day as needed to relieve cold symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat the common cold and flu symptoms. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce congestion, soothe a sore throat, and alleviate coughs. The bioactive constituents of yarrow tea, including flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and phenolic acids, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the growth of pathogens and reducing inflammation. Drinking Achillea millefolium teas regularly may help to relieve cold and flu symptoms, promote recovery, and provide relief from respiratory issues.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers. Rinse with clean water.
Dry the leaves and flowers completely using a paper towel or a clean cloth.
Measure 2 tablespoons of dried Achillea millefolium and place it in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dried Achillea millefolium. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it hot or let it cool down before consuming. Repeat 2-3 times a day as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat colds, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with certain medications. preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint tea, have been traditionally used to treat colds due to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint tea helps to break down mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion, while its analgesic properties soothe sore throats and headaches associated with colds. The bioactive constituents of peppermint tea, including menthol, menthone, and limonene, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing the severity of cold symptoms. The benefits of using peppermint tea to treat colds include rapid relief from congestion and sinus pressure, a soothing effect on the throat and sinuses, and a boost to the immune system to aid in the recovery from the illness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh peppermint leaves (Mentha x piperita) and a large pot.
Boil 2 cups of water and pour it over the peppermint leaves.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a separate container.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea to make it sweeter, if desired.
Drink 1 cup of the peppermint tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve cold symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Preventive nutrition and food science, Mentha x piperita teas for colds may provide antioxidant benefits due to their high antioxidant capacity of 145.99 μmol Trolox/g, which can contribute to their potential therapeutic use when consumed.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be beneficial in treating colds, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its high menthol content. To minimize potential issues, take precautions such as drinking in moderation, avoiding consumption before bedtime, and monitoring body sensitivity to menthol, especially if you have a history of digestive problems.

preparations.herb_desc: Origanum vulgare teas, also known as wild marjoram, have been used for centuries to treat the common cold and its associated symptoms. The antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to combat the infection and reduce congestion, making it easier to breathe and recover from the illness. The bioactive constituents of Origanum vulgare, including carvacrol and thymol, have been shown to have potent antioxidant and immune-boosting effects, which aid in the treatment of colds by reducing oxidative stress and supporting the body's natural defense mechanisms. Regular consumption of Origanum vulgare teas has been found to provide relief from cold symptoms, such as coughing, sneezing, and congestion, and can also help to prevent the onset of secondary infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Origanum vulgare leaves.
Steep 2 tablespoons of the leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, for sweetness.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve cold symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Origanum vulgare teas, which have high phenolic compounds content and antioxidant properties, may be beneficial in treating colds.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can be used to treat colds, but consuming it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize risks, it is recommended to start with small amounts, monitor your body's reaction, and avoid consuming it if you have sensitive stomach or allergies, and also be cautious when using it during pregnancy or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been traditionally used to treat colds and alleviate symptoms associated with this ailment. The properties of Melissa officinalis teas help to treat colds by exhibiting antiviral and anti-inflammatory activities, which can reduce the severity and duration of the illness. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis teas, including rosmarinic acid, eugenol, and beta-caryophyllene, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the replication of viruses and modulating the immune response. The benefits of Melissa officinalis teas in treating colds include reduced congestion, coughing, and fatigue, as well as improved sleep quality and overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of boiling water in a pot.
Add the Melissa leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea hot or cold, 2-3 times a day to help relieve cold symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Melissa officinalis teas for colds have antioxidant properties with a high phenolic compounds content, specifically with a TEAC of 4.06+/-0.31 mM and QE of 1370.09+/-41.38 microM.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be an effective remedy for colds, but its use may be associated with side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize potential risks, it's essential to consume Melissa officinalis teas in moderation, avoid taking it with other medications, and monitor your body's response for any adverse reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, also known as dandelion tea, have been traditionally used to treat colds and other respiratory issues. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties, particularly the flavonoids and terpenoids present in it, help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the sinuses, making it easier to breathe. The bioactive constituents, such as sesquiterpene lactones, have been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which aid in fighting off infections that cause colds. Drinking dandelion tea can provide relief from cold symptoms, such as cough, sore throat, and fever, and may also help to boost the immune system.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot over low heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers to the water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink the tea hot or cold, up to 3 times a day.

preparations.herb_desc: Ulmus rubra teas have been traditionally used to treat colds due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the severity of symptoms such as congestion and cough. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids that work synergistically to combat the underlying causes of the common cold. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting the growth of pathogens, Ulmus rubra teas help to alleviate symptoms and promote recovery. The benefits of using Ulmus rubra teas to treat colds include faster recovery time, reduced risk of complications, and improved overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Ulmus rubra leaves and flowers. Clean them with water.
Heat 2 cups of water in a pot over medium heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of Ulmus rubra leaves and flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help relieve cold symptoms.

To use Ulmus rubra teas safely, it's essential to start with a low dose, gradually increase the amount, and avoid consuming it in excess, especially when combined with other medications or herbal remedies.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea pallida teas have been traditionally used to treat colds and flu due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents, including alkylamides, glycoproteins, and phenolic acids, help to stimulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms such as fever and congestion. By boosting the body's natural defenses and reducing the severity of cold symptoms, Echinacea pallida teas provide relief and support the body's recovery process. The benefits of using Echinacea pallida teas to treat colds include reduced duration and severity of symptoms, improved immune function, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Echinacea pallida roots and flowers.
Add the dried Echinacea pallida to 1 cup of boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help with cold symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Zeitschrift fur arztliche Fortbildung, Echinacea pallida teas for colds may be effective due to the presence of pharmacologically active compounds, such as polysaccharides, glycoproteins, caffeic acid derivatives, and alkamides, particularly in alcoholic extracts of the roots.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea pallida teas can be used to help alleviate symptoms of the common cold, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. To use Echinacea pallida teas safely, take them in moderation and avoid using them for extended periods, especially if you have sensitive digestive systems or allergies.

preparation_study: According to Phytomedicine: international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology, Echinacea purpurea teas for colds are effective alternatives to standard symptomatic medicines, with Echinaforce and its concentrated preparation being significantly more effective than a special Echinacea extract or placebo in reducing the complaint index of 12 symptoms during common cold.
intro_study: According to "Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y)", teas for colds, such as Echinacea Plus, have been found to be effective in relieving symptoms and reducing the duration of colds when consumed at the early onset of symptoms.
